name: Vegetable Ladder Loaf
recipeIngredient: 2 medium red, yellow, or orange sweet peppers
1/4 cup pine nuts or slivered almonds, toasted
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast the peppers until skin is charred and remove the skin. Cut into strips.
2. In a food processor, combine spinach, basil, Parmesan cheese, garlic, and 1 tablespoon of beaten egg. Pulse until well combined.
3. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or plain brown paper. Unfold sheets of pastry on the baking sheets. Place one sheet of pastry on a baking sheet. Brush the pastry lightly with beaten egg. Sprinkle half of bread crumbs in a 3-inch strip down the center of the pastry. Spread half of spinach-basil mixture on top, within one inch of the ends. Sprinkle with half of the toasted nuts and arrange pepper strips evenly over the filling.
4. Make 3-inch cuts from the edges toward the center at 1-inch intervals on both sides of the filling. Starting at one end, alternately fold opposite strips of dough across the filling.
5. Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
6. Brush each pastry piece with beaten egg.
7. Bake at 425°F for 25 minutes or until puffed and golden brown. Serve warm. Cut each pastry crosswise into six slices, then cut each slice in half to make 24 pieces.
